coin_profile.name: OKB
coin_profile.bucket_explanation: A Cash-Flow Protocol is a blockchain or app that earns real money from its users — just like a business. Every time someone makes a trade, borrows money, or uses the network, it collects a small fee. The more people use it, the more fees it earns. We value these tokens similarly to how you would value a business — by looking at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it.
coin_profile.description: OKB (OKB) — a Cash-Flow Protocol ranked #46 by market cap.

dilution_control.label: Dilution Control
dilution_control.chart_ref: Network Economics → Token Supply: Issuance vs Burns

chart_zones.mos_note: Cycle phase (Insufficient Data) not favourable for entry — wait for accumulation or early uptrend.

2026.net_issuance_note: Current snapshot only. Historical supply data requires on-chain indexing not available via free APIs.

meta.data_note: No protocol fee or TVL data found for this token on DeFiLlama. Charts 1 and 2 are unavailable. Charts 3 and 4 show current-year supply and dilution data from CoinGecko.

forecast_meta.growth_basis_note: Annual growth rates derived from quality score 5.3/10 and survivability 70.0%. Base: 7.0% p.a., Bull: 13.0% p.a., Bear: -21.1% p.a.
forecast_meta.method_explanation: How we valued this token: We treat this like a small business. It earns fees every time someone uses it, so we look at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it. We combine up to three simple checks: (1) How busy is the network? More activity usually means more value. (2) Is the market cap cheap or expensive compared to the fees it earns? (3) What price would make sense given its size rank? The Speculative Fair Value is the average of whichever checks produced a sensible result.
forecast_meta.growth_explanation: Base (7.0%/yr): projected growth based on this token's quality score (5.3/10). Bull (13.0%/yr): adoption accelerates and network usage grows strongly. Bear (-21.1%/yr): adoption slows or competition erodes the token's position. The bear scenario is steeper than usual because survivability is only 70.0% — this token has weaker dilution control or holder value accrual, making it more vulnerable in a downturn.
forecast_meta.iv_selection_rationale: Bucket A (Cash-Flow Protocol): Up to 7 methods are available — Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, Holder Revenue, Metcalfe Network Value, Network Activity (NVT), Rank Benchmark, and Dilution Adjustment. Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, and Holder Revenue are unavailable: this token has no DeFiLlama fee tracking. Valuation relies entirely on network activity and market positioning signals. Network Activity (NVT) did not contribute to the median: the implied fair value fell outside the 0.1x–10x price bounds (volume is very low or very high relative to circulating supply). Rank Benchmark anchors to the expected market cap for this token's rank tier, quality-adjusted and capped at 2× current price to avoid distortion. Dilution Adjustment did not fire: FDV/MC is within the acceptable range for this quality tier, so no dilution penalty was applied. Only 1 method contributed to the median — the valuation should be treated as a rough anchor, not a precise fair value. Methods producing values outside 0.01x–15x of current price are excluded as outliers. The mean of all qualifying per-token estimates becomes the Speculative Fair Value.
forecast_meta.price_history_note: Historical price data unavailable for this token. Forecast chart shows projected values only.

coin_profile.description: Optimism is an Ethereum Layer 2 using optimistic rollup technology. It pioneered the Superchain vision — a network of OP Stack chains including Coinbase's Base. meta.data_note: No protocol fee or TVL data found for this token on DeFiLlama. Charts 1 and 2 are unavailable. Charts 3 and 4 show current-year supply and dilution data from CoinGecko.

forecast_meta.growth_basis_note: Annual growth rates derived from quality score 3.2/10 and survivability 26.7%. Base: 3.0% p.a., Bull: 9.0% p.a., Bear: -37.0% p.a.
forecast_meta.method_explanation: How we valued this token: We treat this like a small business. It earns fees every time someone uses it, so we look at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it. We combine up to three simple checks: (1) How busy is the network? More activity usually means more value. (2) Is the market cap cheap or expensive compared to the fees it earns? (3) What price would make sense given its size rank? The Speculative Fair Value is the average of whichever checks produced a sensible result.
forecast_meta.growth_explanation: Base (3.0%/yr): projected growth based on this token's quality score (3.2/10). Bull (9.0%/yr): adoption accelerates and network usage grows strongly. Bear (-37.0%/yr): adoption slows or competition erodes the token's position. The bear scenario is steeper than usual because survivability is only 26.7% — this token has weaker dilution control or holder value accrual, making it more vulnerable in a downturn.
forecast_meta.iv_selection_rationale: Bucket A (Cash-Flow Protocol): Up to 7 methods are available — Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, Holder Revenue, Metcalfe Network Value, Network Activity (NVT), Rank Benchmark, and Dilution Adjustment. Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, and Holder Revenue are unavailable: this token has no DeFiLlama fee tracking. Valuation relies entirely on network activity and market positioning signals. Network Activity (NVT) did not contribute to the median: the implied fair value fell outside the 0.1x–10x price bounds (volume is very low or very high relative to circulating supply). Rank Benchmark anchors to the expected market cap for this token's rank tier, quality-adjusted and capped at 2× current price to avoid distortion. Dilution Adjustment penalises the large gap between circulating and fully-diluted supply — future token unlocks are expected to create selling pressure. Only 2 methods contributed to the median. More data sources (DeFiLlama fee history) would improve confidence in this estimate. Methods producing values outside 0.01x–15x of current price are excluded as outliers. The mean of all qualifying per-token estimates becomes the Speculative Fair Value.
forecast_meta.price_history_note: Limited price history available (1 year(s)). This token may be recently listed or have insufficient on-chain data.

bull_points: Current market fear environment may create a near-term mean reversion opportunity in ONFA
Any positive news catalyst — exchange listing, partnership, or protocol upgrade — could trigger a sharp near-term rally from current levels
Crypto market sentiment is cyclical — ONFA's short-term setup may improve as macro uncertainty fades
bear_points: Up 24% in the past week — rapid short-term move increases likelihood of a pullback before the next leg higher
Regulatory developments in major markets could create short-term selling pressure
Without a near-term catalyst, ONFA may continue to consolidate or drift lower

bull_points: Long-term adoption thesis for Cash-Flow Protocol assets remains structurally intact
As the crypto ecosystem matures, ONFA's established position should compound in value
Continued development activity and ecosystem growth support a constructive long-term view
bear_points: No multi-year fee data exists for ONFA — the protocol's long-term economic durability remains unproven
FDV is 2.8x market cap — substantial token unlock pressure over the coming years could persistently weigh on price
Quality score of 3.2/10 (Dangerous) signals multiple structural concerns that could compound over a long horizon

coin_profile.name: Orbs
coin_profile.bucket_explanation: A Cash-Flow Protocol is a blockchain or app that earns real money from its users — just like a business. Every time someone makes a trade, borrows money, or uses the network, it collects a small fee. The more people use it, the more fees it earns. We value these tokens similarly to how you would value a business — by looking at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it.

forecast_meta.method_explanation: How we valued this token: We treat this like a small business. It earns fees every time someone uses it, so we look at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it. We combine up to three simple checks: (1) How busy is the network? forecast_meta.iv_selection_rationale: Bucket A (Cash-Flow Protocol): Up to 7 methods are available — Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, Holder Revenue, Metcalfe Network Value, Network Activity (NVT), Rank Benchmark, and Dilution Adjustment. Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, and Holder Revenue are unavailable: this token has no DeFiLlama fee tracking. Valuation relies entirely on network activity and market positioning signals. Network Activity (NVT) treats 24h trading volume as a proxy for on-chain utility — it fires when volume is available and the resulting IV falls within 0.1x–10x of current price. Metcalfe Network Value (MC/Volume ratio) confirmed the NVT signal and contributed to the median pool. Rank Benchmark anchors to the expected market cap for this token's rank tier, quality-adjusted and capped at 2× current price to avoid distortion. Dilution Adjustment penalises the large gap between circulating and fully-diluted supply — future token unlocks are expected to create selling pressure. Methods producing values outside 0.01x–15x of current price are excluded as outliers. The mean of all qualifying per-token estimates becomes the Speculative Fair Value.
